cases.prompt_text:
Patient: 45 F
Indication: Aspergilloma
Airway: Tracheostomy
Procedure: Injection of Voriconazole (50mg)
Target: Left Lower Lobe (LLL)
Findings: thick tan secretions

Prepare an operative report for a 96 year old female undergoing bronchoscopy for airway obstruction. Note a complication: EBUS caused a tear in the distal tracheal posterior membrane. GI consult ruled out esophageal injury. Initial studded silicone stent failed because migration occurred immediately upon deployment requiring removal. Successful placement of straight silicone stent measuring 18mm x 50mm and dilation to 18 mm.
required_markdown_contains: tear in the distal tracheal posterior membrane

cases.prompt_text: Please document IP bronchoscopy. Codes 31645, 31640, 31641 with modifier 22. Patient required switch from LMA to 7.5mm ETT due to bleeding. Bronchus Intermedius opened from 10% to 40%.
required_markdown_contains: Bronchus intermedius obstruction requiring therapeutic bronchoscopy
switch from LMA to 7.5mm ETT due to bleeding

cases.prompt_text:
Patient: 80 M
Indication: large thyroid mass / ~10 days intubation
Procedure: Rigid Bronch, EBUS, BAL (Right Middle Lobe (RML))
Findings: Stenosis length 4.0 cm, Left VC erythema
Outcome: Tracheostomy by ENT

PATIENT: 58/F
INDICATION: post-transplant anastomotic stricture
PROCEDURE: Rigid Bronchoscopy, Stent Exchange (Left), Balloon Dilation (Right), BAL.
FINDINGS: Web-like stenosis at the right bronchial anastomosis; Stent in place at LMSB with thick mucus throughout.
DETAILS: Stent Ultraflex 12 x 40mm; Balloon Elation.
required_markdown_contains: Airway Stent Removal / Revision (Left Main Stem (LMS) bronchus)

cases.prompt_text: Please generate a rigid bronchoscopy report. Patient is a 58 year old female with recurrent granulation tissue. We found a 80% obstruction in the Left Lower Lobe. Used APC and mechanical debulking to open it. Placed a Bonastent 10x20 stent in the LLL basal subsegment. Also did a BAL of the Left Upper Lobe.

Please generate an IP op note for a 59 year old female with mediastinal adenopathy and pulmonary nodules. We did a BAL at the Lateral Segment of RML and EBBX at multiple carinas. Used radial EBUS for a LLL lesion, taking biopsies. Staged mediastinum, sampled Station 7 which was Type 3, skipped the smaller nodes.
required_markdown_contains: Bronchoalveolar lavage (BAL) Lateral Segment of RML

cases.prompt_text: Pt: 65 M. S/p Ivor Lewis with BPF (2.0 cm, distal left mainstem). Proc: Bronch, Biopsy, Stenting. Complication: 14x30 mm pushed distal by 14x40 mm. Action: Removed 14x40 mm via stoma (trach exchange), retracted 14x30 mm. Plan: ICU, nebs Q6H.

Bronchoscopy report. 59yo M. Indication: Lung nodule. Procedure: Ion nav bronch to LUL Apicoposterior Segment (LB1+2) + EBUS. Findings: 0.8 cm Concentric nodule. Sampling: TBNA x5, TBBX x4, BAL. EBUS sampled Station 4R. Fiducial placed. No comps.
required_markdown_contains: Robotic navigational bronchoscopy (Ion) to LUL target
Transbronchial needle aspiration of LUL (5 passes)
Transbronchial biopsy of LUL target (4 samples)

cases.prompt_text: Please write a procedure note for a 72 year old female with a Parapneumonic Effusion. We performed a bedside ultrasound and subsequent fibrinolytic instillation on the left. Ultrasound showed Small volume with Thick loculations. We gave dose number 6 of tPA / DNase.
required_markdown_contains: Left intrapleural fibrinolytic instillation via existing pleural drain
Thoracic ultrasound of the left hemithorax

cases.prompt_text: 41yo F D14.31. Ion bronch RUL 1.5 cm lesion. tortuous airway anatomy so used balloon. EBUS 7 Sampled. rose conclusive for adenocarcinoma. no comps.
required_markdown_contains: Robotic navigational bronchoscopy (Ion) to RUL target
Balloon support was used due to tortuous airway anatomy.

cases.prompt_text:
PROCEDURE: Robotic Bronchoscopy (Ion) & EBUS.
PATIENT: 81 M.
TARGETS: RLL (RB6) & RLL (RB9).
NODE: Station 4L.
RESULT: Adenocarcinoma.
required_markdown_contains: Robotic navigational bronchoscopy (Ion) to 2 targets (RLL (RB6), RLL (RB9))
RLL (RB6) and RLL (RB9) targets successfully sampled via robotic bronchoscopy.

cases.prompt_text: Bronchoscopy note for 62yo F. Dx: Airway stenosis (Right). Findings: ~5mm stenosis RMSB, malacia moderate. BAL performed RLL BAL.

Patient: 71 M
Surgeon: Dr. Chen
Procedure: Bronchoscopy with Stent Removal & Cryo
Key Findings:
- LMSB stent completely occluded by inspissated secretions
- blood-tinged mucus plugs
- LUL BAL performed
Plan: Admit, polymicrobial infection, Unasyn and Dexamethasone 4 mg.
required_markdown_contains: Airway Stent Removal / Revision (Left Main Stem (LMS) bronchus)
Admit, polymicrobial infection, Unasyn and Dexamethasone 4 mg

cases.prompt_text:
Patient: 52 M
Indication: transplant complications/stenosis
RML Procedure: Dilation, BAL, 6mm x 22mm stent.
Lingula Procedure: Attempted 7mm x 16mm stent (failed/removed), balloon dilation.
Debridement: RMS using Cryotherapy.
Complications: no active bleeding, minimal oozing
required_markdown_contains: Right Main Stem (RMS) bronchus was treated with a cryoprobe
forbidden_markdown_contains: Endobronchial obstruction at RML was treated with a cryoprobe

cases.prompt_text: 47yo M w/ complicated effusion. right-sided tube. gave tpa/dnase initial dose. code 32561. no issues.
required_markdown_contains: Right intrapleural fibrinolytic instillation via existing pleural drain

cases.prompt_text: Procedure codes 76604 and 32562. 64 year old female. Dx: Empyema. left hemithorax US findings: Large volume. Meds: 5 mg tPA / 5 mg DNase. Comps: None.
required_markdown_contains: Left intrapleural fibrinolytic instillation via existing pleural drain
Thoracic ultrasound of the left hemithorax

cases.prompt_text: Procedures: Bronchoscopy, Stent Placement (Aero 14x20 mm, 12x40 mm), Trach Exchange. Diagnosis: BPF post-esophagectomy. Indication: 1.5 cm fistula in proximal left mainstem. Complication: Stent migration requiring revision. Disposition: ICU.

Please generate an operative report for a 56 year old male referred by Dr. Banerji. Patient has bilateral transplant complications. We did a BAL, RML stenting with a 7mm x 19mm iCast fully covered, and attempted Lingula stenting which failed and required removal. We used the Erbe for RMS debridement. Note the complex dual scope work.
required_markdown_contains: Performed at the Right Main Stem (RMS) bronchus
attempted Lingula stenting which failed and required removal

cases.prompt_text: Procedures: Bronchoscopy, Stent Placement (Aero 14x20 mm, 12x40 mm), Trach Exchange. Diagnosis: BPF post-esophagectomy. Indication: 1.0 cm fistula in proximal left mainstem. Complication: Stent migration requiring revision. Disposition: ICU.
